title: Post-Qualification Requirements. Upon receipt of the notification, the
content: 20 applicant shall submit, prior to the issuance of the provisional license, the following post 21 qualification requirements as may be prescribed by the Department: 22 23 a. A lease contract for an office space measuring at least one hundred (100) square 24 meters, or proof of building ownership that will be used as an office or an identified 25 portion thereof measuring at least 100 square meters; 26 27 b. An office layout providing and clearly delineating adequate spaces that will serve as 28 a receiving area, interview room, administrative and finance area, 29 conference/training room, and an executive office; 30 31 c. An inventory of office equipment and facilities, which at the minimum, shall include 32 the following: 33 34 i. Office furniture such as tables and chairs for the president, other officers and 35 employees, as well as similar furniture for the receiving area, interview room 36 and conference/training room; 37 38 ii. Secured filing cabinets; and 39 40 iii. Office equipment, which at the minimum, shall include two (2) computers, 41 computer printer and scanner, photocopying machine, landline telephone, 42 internet connection, and multi-media equipment (i.e. LCD projector, and other 43 similar audio-visual equipment). 44 45 d. Organizational chart indicating the duties and responsibilities and names of officers 46 and staff, which at the minimum, shall consist of the President, Chief Executive 47 Officer, Recruitment and/or Documentation Officer, Accountant or Bookkeeper, 48 Cashier, and Liaison Officer; 49 50 e. An escrow agreement with a bank authorized by the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas 51 (BSP) to handle trust accounts, with deposit in the amount not less than One Million 52 Five Hundred Thousand Pesos (PhP 1,500,000.00); 53 54 f. Updated bank certificate stating that the escrow deposit remains at One Million Five 55 Hundred Thousand Pesos (PhP 1,500,000.00); 56 57 g. A duly notarized undertaking by the sole proprietor, the managing partner, or the 58 president of the corporation, stating that the applicant shall: 59 60 i. Select and deploy only medically fit and technically qualified recruits; 61 62 12 ii. Assume full and complete responsibility for all claims and liabilities which may 1 arise in connection with the use of the license; 2 3 iii. Assume joint and several liability with the employer/shipowner/principal for all 4 claims and liabilities which may arise in connection with the implementation of 5 the contract, including but not limited to unpaid wages, death and disability 6 compensation and repatriation; 7 8 iv. Guarantee compliance with existing labor and social legislation of the 9 Philippines and applicable regulations of the Flag State and international 10 organizations such as the International Maritime Organization (IMO) and 11 International Labour Organization (ILO); 12 13 v. Assume full and complete responsibility for all valid, legal and authorized acts 14 of its officers, employees and representatives done in connection with 15 recruitment and placement; 16 17 vi. Negotiate for the best terms and conditions of employment for its seafarers; 18 19 vii. Inform the seafarers of their rights and duties and disclose the full terms and 20 conditions of employment under their contracts of employment and articles of 21 agreement prior to and in the process of engagement; 22 23 viii. Provide orientation to the seafarers on recruitment procedures, terms and 24 conditions and other relevant information to its seafarers, and provide the 25 necessary facilities for the purpose; 26 27 ix. Act on complaints or problems brought to its attention or submit reports on the 28 status or condition of seafarers; 29 30 x. Ensure that the ship and the crew are adequately insured by the Protection and 31 Indemnity Club or similar insurance, inclusive of the compulsory insurance 32 required under R.A. No. 8042, as amended, through the submission of a 33 Certificate of Cover; 34 35 xi. Ensure that the contracts of employment are in accordance with the standard 36 employment contract and other applicable laws, regulations and collective 37 bargaining agreements; 38 39 xii. Guarantee that there is no officer or employee of the licensed manning agency 40 related within the fourth civil degree of consanguinity or affinity to any official or 41 employee of any government agency engaged, directly or indirectly, in the 42 implementation of R.A. No. 8042, as amended; 43 44 xiii. Adhere to ethical standards as prescribed in the Code of Conduct for Ethical 45 Recruitment, as endorsed by the manning industry associations and the 46 Department; 47 48 xiv. Repatriate deployed seafarers and their personal belongings when the need 49 arises or reimburse expenses of repatriation advanced by OWWA; 50 51 xv. Deploy at least seventy-five (75) seafarers to a new principal and its ship/s 52 within three (3) years from the issuance of its license; and 53 54 h. Proof of appointment of a Data Privacy Officer in compliance with R.A. No. 10173 55 and submission of non-disclosure agreements signed by each 56 staff/personnel/employees/officer of licensed manning agency pursuant to Section 57 39 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. No. 11641. 58 59 The Department, in accordance with law and after consultation with the stakeholders, 60 may modify the list or prescribe additional post-qualification requirements. 61 62 13
